author: Garbage
text:
Reconstruct closed tasks from state-branch history

Closed tasks are git-rm'd from the balls/tasks orphan branch by Store::close_and_archive, so they survived only in that branch's history with no command able to read them back: bl show <id> hit TaskNotFound and bl list --all was a dead flag filtering an on-disk scan that never contains closed files.

New src/archive_recovery.rs is the single read-only engine. It walks the state branch's --diff-filter=D log, reads each task's blob at the commit before its deletion, and overlays the authoritative close facts. Subtlety baked into the module doc: close_and_archive never re-saves the task before the git rm, so the recovered blob is the pre-close (review-state) snapshot; status=Closed and closed_at come from the deletion commit, not the file. Nothing else is fabricated, so --json stays faithful and bl show's delivered: line still resolves the permanent [bl-id] tag on main.

Surfaces: bl show <id> falls back to recovery on TaskNotFound (no new flag — this fixes the promise its own help text already made for closed tasks). bl list gains --closed (closed only) and --all is made real (open + closed together); --status closed is an alias. Sets that may carry closed tasks render flat because the grouped view deliberately omits Closed. Stealth/no-git stores can't recover (no state branch) and say so without failing.

Pure parser split out for full branch coverage; 13 unit + 6 integration tests; clippy clean; 100% line coverage; README and SKILL.md updated.
